plan.action: Load dataset and perform initial EDA: check shape, dtypes, first few rows. Analyze target distribution (days of rental occupancy) for skewness/outliers.
Check for missing values across all columns and identify data types (numeric, categorical, date objects).
Identify date-related columns; extract temporal features (year, month, day, weekday, season) if present. Plot target vs date features.
Handle missing values: impute numeric with median/mean, categorical with mode or create indicator. Encode categorical variables (one-hot or label encoding).
Create derived features: interaction terms, polynomial features for key numeric columns, binning of continuous variables if useful.
Split data into train/validation (e.g., 80/20). Set up MSE-based cross-validation (5-fold) for model evaluation.
Train baseline Ridge Regression: quick linear baseline to compare against.
Train Random Forest regressor: tune n_estimators and max_depth using CV MSE.
Train Gradient Boosting (LightGBM or XGBoost): tune learning_rate, n_estimators, max_depth using CV MSE. Start with LightGBM per best practices.
Compare all models by CV MSE; select best performer. Document MSE scores for Ridge, RF, and GBM.
Review model performance: check for overfitting (large train vs CV gap), analyze residual plots, identify high-leverage points.
If issues found (high bias/variance), propose remedial actions: feature selection, regularization adjustment, hyperparameter refinement.
Incorporate Critic feedback: retrain with adjusted hyperparameters or refined features if needed.
Final training: retrain best model on full dataset (all training data) with optimized hyperparameters.
Generate predictions on test set (if test.csv exists) and create submission file with predicted days of rental occupancy.
